skill.name: internationalizing-websites
skill.description: Adds internationalization support to Next.js websites using next-intl, localized JSON files, hreflang guidance, sitemap updates, and international SEO checks.
skill.summary: Internationalize Next.js sites with locale files, hreflang setup, sitemap updates, and SEO validation.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns. Review found no prompt injection, obfuscation, credential exfiltration, or confirmed malicious intent; most high alerts are documentation examples or false positives. The real risk is moderate because bundled scripts intentionally rewrite local Next.js i18n, locale, request, and sitemap files.

medium_findings.title: Local Project File Rewrites
medium_findings.description: The language automation script copies translation files and rewrites locale, request, and sitemap files in the target project. This is expected for the skill, but it can overwrite project configuration if run without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script uses direct fs.copyFileSync, fs.readFileSync, and fs.writeFileSync calls against project paths. The behavior is intentional and local, but the write impact is clear.

medium_findings.title: Translation Schema Script Writes Relative Project Paths
medium_findings.description: The schema script writes localized schema and pricing blocks into ../i18n/messages files for hardcoded locales. The locale list is fixed, so traversal is not confirmed, but project files are modified in place.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write path includes a parent-directory segment, but locale values come from a hardcoded array. This supports a moderate local-write risk, not a confirmed traversal vulnerability.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Examples Trigger Static Alerts
low_findings.description: Many external command detections are Markdown examples such as node, npm, curl, grep, cat, ls, and code commands. They are user-visible instructions, not hidden execution by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are fenced Markdown snippets or checklist instructions. I found no evidence that these documentation snippets execute automatically.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Examples And References
low_findings.description: The network detections are example hreflang URLs, validation links, and documentation references. The executable scripts do not make network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in Markdown examples, SEO references, or curl commands the user may run. No fetch, HTTP client, or network module use was found in the scripts reviewed.

low_findings.title: High Static Alerts Are Mostly Semantic False Positives
low_findings.description: Weak-crypto and sensitive-data alerts match language codes, Markdown terms, browser storage guidance, and an example missing.key string. These do not show cryptographic use or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations show terminology or regex iteration, including entryRegex.exec on local content. I did not find evidence of crypto primitives, secret collection, or credential exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Synchronous Filesystem Mutation
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts synchronously read, copy, and write target project files. Users should inspect changes before committing them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: These exact lines perform fs copy or write operations. They are not malicious by themselves, but they are the concrete risky pattern in this skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell And Network Validation Commands In Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow asks users to run local shell commands and curl checks. These commands are transparent examples and should be adapted to trusted project domains.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible documentation, not hidden execution. The risk is limited to users running them in their own project environment.

content.value_statement: Adding languages to a Next.js site can break routing, SEO, and translation files. This skill provides scripts, checklists, and references for structured i18n rollout.
actual_capabilities: Creates new language JSON files from English templates for configured locale directories.
Updates next-intl locale and request configuration patterns for supported languages.
Regenerates sitemap hreflang entries for localized URL versions.
Adds localized schema and pricing translations for predefined languages.
Provides hreflang, locale code, and international SEO reference checklists.
Guides manual validation for language switching, metadata, and crawlability.
limitations: The included scripts assume a specific Next.js and next-intl project structure.
Translations still require native speaker review for quality and cultural fit.
The scripts can modify project files in place and should be reviewed before commit.
Some example translations are tailored to a Word2MD product and may need replacement.

use_cases.title: Launch A Multilingual SaaS Site
use_cases.description: Add Japanese, Chinese, Korean, and European language files to an existing Next.js product site.

use_cases.title: Audit International SEO Setup
use_cases.description: Check hreflang tags, localized sitemaps, metadata, and Search Console readiness before launch.

use_cases.title: Standardize Translation Workflows
use_cases.description: Create repeatable steps for adding locales, reviewing translations, and validating language routes.

prompt_templates.title: Add One Language
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add Japanese support to my Next.js site. Inspect my i18n structure first and explain each file change.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Global Locale Rollout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan adding ja, zh, ko, fr, de, and es to my next-intl project. Identify config, sitemap, and translation tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Review Hreflang SEO
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to audit my hreflang implementation, localized sitemap, html lang attributes, and metadata for international SEO issues.

prompt_templates.title: Customize Automation Scripts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to adapt the i18n scripts for my custom locale directories. Add safeguards and show a dry-run style change summary.

output_examples.input: Add Korean and Japanese to my existing Next.js app.
output: A list of existing English translation templates found in the project.
A proposed set of new Korean and Japanese translation files.
A summary of locale, request, and sitemap updates to review.

output_examples.input: Check whether my multilingual site is ready for launch.
output: An international SEO checklist covering hreflang, sitemap, metadata, and Search Console tasks.
A list of missing or inconsistent language routes.
Recommended fixes ordered by launch risk.

output_examples.input: Help me localize structured data for French and German pages.
output: A review of existing schema translation keys.
Localized schema content areas that need human review.
A concise validation plan for rich result testing.

best_practices: Run scripts on a clean branch and review every generated file diff before committing.
Research keywords in each target language instead of directly translating English terms.
Validate hreflang, metadata, routing, and character rendering for every published locale.
anti_patterns: Do not publish machine translations without native speaker or market review.
Do not force IP-based redirects that block users or crawlers from other language pages.
Do not mix unrelated regional codes, country codes, and language codes in URLs.

faq.question: Does this skill translate all website content automatically?
faq.answer: No. It helps create files and workflows, but translation quality still needs human review.

faq.question: Which framework does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Next.js projects that use next-intl and JSON translation files.

faq.question: Can it update my sitemap?
faq.answer: Yes. The language script can regenerate sitemap entries with hreflang links for configured locales.

faq.question: Is it safe to run the included scripts?
faq.answer: They are local project scripts, but they modify files. Run them on a branch and review the diff.

faq.question: Does it support right-to-left languages?
faq.answer: The references include RTL guidance for Arabic and similar languages. Implementation still requires layout testing.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
